title: Standard Library Problem Solving · Failure Triage
summary: Add time, statistics, secure identifiers, text search, process, and logging features to the data tool. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a standard-library operations toolkit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use date, time, datetime, timedelta, and timezone-aware values without comparing naive and aware timestamps.
Use math and statistics functions with explicit domain, empty-input, precision, and outlier assumptions.
Use random for simulation and secrets for security-sensitive tokens while controlling reproducibility where appropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
